_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: User focus involves understanding the user needs to develop a detailed understanding of the problems that need to be solved.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-S-001 (DG, cosine: 0.340), AB-S-006 (AB, cosine: 0.338).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.200). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DDaT-only terms [detailed, develop, focus, involves, needs, problems] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.864, R=0.851). Balanced P/R — the 20-word DDaT element and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (97.3%). "User focus involves understanding the user needs to develop a detailed understan" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.382 > STS 0.200 (gap +0.182). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.3%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DDaT terms: [detailed, develop, focus, involves, needs, problems]. WIDAI terms: [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.182 with low STS 0.200. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
